personal.name: Ángel Roldán Rabanal
personal.title: Multiplatform & Web Developer · DAM
personal.tagline: Multiplatform & web developer — I like creative, efficient, user-focused applications (same line as my GitHub profile). Mostly PHP, SQL and JavaScript in production; this site is a Next.js playground. Seville; Erasmus+ in Prague. Always learning and exploring new technologies.
personal.closing: Driving licence · fine with travel when it makes sense — write if you want to compare notes or talk roles.

about: Multiplatform & web developer — user-centred delivery is the thread through my GitHub and my day jobs. Higher Vocational student in Multiplatform Application Development (SAFA), with a few years of real tickets, queries and deploys behind me. I like systems that stay understandable: backends you can reason about, front-ends that do not fight the user, and integrations you can revisit months later without dread. Erasmus+ in Prague is the latest chapter — more AI-assisted workflows, same curiosity about how things actually run in production.

projects.title: This portfolio (open source)
projects.description: The site you are on: Next.js 16, React Three Fiber, GSAP, Lenis, EN/ES i18n. Source on GitHub; typically deployed on Vercel.

projects.title: TECHNOAPP
projects.description: TypeScript UI shipped on Vercel. Repository: github.com/aroldanrabanal/TECHNOAPP

projects.title: CampusGo
projects.description: Web client (TypeScript) and Android app in Java. Repos: github.com/aroldanrabanal/CampusGo and github.com/aroldanrabanal/AppCampusGo.

experience.description: Erasmus+ internship in Prague: internal and client-facing tools with a lot of AI-assisted delivery — automation, leaner iterations, hybrid cadence with the team.
Maintenance and small features on PHP/SQL/JS stacks — performance passes, UX fixes, the unglamorous work that keeps a music-industry app humming.
Institutional web work: custom PHP, tuned MySQL, Google and REST integrations — the kind of full-stack glue where the brief changes mid-semester and the data model has to keep up.

education.degree: Higher Vocational Degree — Multiplatform App Development

hero.kicker: Seville ↔ Prague — institutional PHP stacks, music-industry maintenance, and whatever I am breaking in Next.js after hours. Nothing here is a sales deck.
hero.stats: Code in production since 2023 · Erasmus+ Prague, 2026

heading: A bit
about me
body: I am Ángel — multiplatform and web dev path at SAFA (Seville), aligned with what I publish on GitHub: steady work on institutional and music-industry PHP/SQL/JS codebases. I care about boring reliability: migrations you can replay, queries you can explain, and UIs that do not surprise people who already have a full calendar.
Prague (Buinsoft) is where I am leaning into AI-assisted delivery without pretending the fundamentals disappear. This page is partly CV, partly lab log — scroll for selected work and contact.

items.title: Web apps
items.blurb: PHP and SQL first, JS where the browser needs it — CRUD, auth, reports, the unglamorous middle of a product.

items.title: Mobile & Ionic
items.blurb: Shared Angular + Capacitor builds for things that need to leave the desk — still one codebase to reason about.

items.title: Backends & APIs
items.blurb: Services that talk to MySQL/Oracle without mystery — versioning, logging, and contracts you can grep.

items.title: Data modelling
items.blurb: Schemas and migrations that survive the next feature request — not perfect diagrams, tables that behave.

items.title: Integrations
items.blurb: OAuth, webhooks, cron — glue code that should read like a checklist when you come back in six months.

items.title: Front-end lab
items.blurb: This portfolio: Next.js, motion, a bit of WebGL — experiments that keep my JS/React muscles warm outside PHP day jobs.

heading: Selected
work
works.intro: Public repositories and live demos: this portfolio, TECHNOAPP on Vercel, and the CampusGo stack (web + Android). Paid work and the Erasmus+ internship are listed under Process below as employment experience.

heading: How I
work
process.intro: Not a methodology slide — just habits that keep school deadlines and production incidents from colliding too loudly.

steps.title: Read the system
steps.body: Screenshots, logs, who owns which table. I try to know what already works before renaming half of it.

steps.title: Small slices
steps.body: Commits and PRs you can actually review — backend and UI in parallel so nobody stares at a blank page for weeks.

steps.title: Ship, then watch
steps.body: Staging when there is one, backups when there should be, and a quick sanity pass on real data shapes.

steps.title: Leave breadcrumbs
steps.body: README fragments, ticket notes, a line in the changelog — future me (or the next dev) deserves a map.

stack.heading: Stack in rotation
stack.intro: Languages, frameworks and infra I actually grep docs for — the same mix behind school projects, institutional tooling, music-industry tickets, and this Next.js portfolio.

heading: Say
hello
cta.body: If you want to swap notes on school, stacks, or Prague/Seville — email is best. No pitch deck required; I read messages slowly and answer honestly.

footer.tagline: Developer portfolio — not an agency site
